name: Incredible Clam Chowder
directions: Combine the onion, bouillon, cauliflower, oats, oregano and cayenne pepper in a large pot.
Bring to boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently for 60 minutes, uncovered, stirring occasionally.
Using a hand blender, puree until smooth.
Stir in clams and their juice, and adjust the seasonings.
Season with black pepper.

name: New England Clam Chowder (Dairy-Free and Low-Fat)
directions: In a large stockpot, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onions and carrots and saute until softened.
Gradually sift in the flour, stirring thoroughly and scraping bottom of pan.
Very gradually stir in the clam broth, scraping sides and bottom of pan to make sure flour is thoroughly incorporated without clumping.
Stir in rice milk until mixture is smooth.
Add the clams and spices, bring soup to a boil, then cover and reduce heat. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Add diced potatoes, cover and simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve with crushed Saltines.

name: Lactose-Free Clam Chowder!
directions: Drain the canned potatoes.
In a large soup pot on medium low heat margarine, add diced shallots and cook until the shallots are almost clear.
Add flour to butter and mix briskly. Allow mixture to thicken.
Add 2 cups soy milk a little at a time.
Allow soy milk mixture to thicken.
Add clams, juice, potatoes, bottled clam juice and 1/2 cup soy milk to the mixture.
On medium heat bring chowder to a boil.
Turn the temperature to low and allow chowder to simmer for 15 minutes stirring frequently.

name: Yummy Clam Chowder for Food Intolerances
directions: Add bacon to sauce pan and cook on medium low heat until crispy.
Add clam juice from both cans.
Add potatoes.
Cook and cover until potatoes are fork tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Stir occasionally so potatoes won't stick.
Combine 1 cup of rice milk with potato starch or Vance's DariFree.
Add rice milk, the rice milk mix, chicken stock, and dill weed.
Stir together.
Cook for about 30-45 minutes or until thickened.
Add the clams in the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Stir occasionally.

name: Tuesday Night Tom Kha Kai
directions: Fry the lemongrass, ginger/galangal, chilis and green onion tops in the oil until they make your eyes hurt.
Add the coconut milk, once it simmers, add protein of your choice and jalapenos. Add broth if needed.
When it simmers, add the veggies, then the basil and salt (to taste). Turn off the heat.
Stir in fish sauce and lime juice.
